tex:
From this list of numbers, write down

32 \quad 33 \quad 34 \quad 35 \quad 36 \quad 37 \quad 38 \quad 39

(a) a multiple of 8,

(b) a square number,

(c) a prime number.

The diagram shows a cuboid with dimensions 8 cm by 6.5 cm by 4 cm.

Calculate the volume of the cuboid.

Triangle ABC is isosceles with AB = AC and angle ABC = 32°.

Find angle BAC.

A train journey takes 5 hours 54 minutes.

(a) The journey starts at 09 15.

Find the time that the journey ends.

(b) The average speed of the train for this journey is 80 km/h.

Calculate the distance travelled.

Sofia has a bag containing 8 blue beads and 7 red beads only. She takes one bead out of the bag at random and replaces it. She does this 90 times.

Find the number of times she expects to take a red bead.

Without using a calculator, work out $\dfrac{3}{4} - \dfrac{2}{3}$.

You must show all your working and give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.

The bearing of X from Y is 274°.

Calculate the bearing of Y from X.

Calculate the area of the sector of a circle with radius 65 mm and sector angle 42°.

Give your answer in square centimetres.

A solid cylinder has radius 3 cm and height 4.5 cm.

Calculate the total surface area of the cylinder.

$y$ is directly proportional to the cube root of $(x + 3)$.

When $x = 5$, $y = \dfrac{2}{3}$.

Find $y$ when $x = 24$.

The total perimeter of a semicircle is 19.02 cm.

Calculate the radius of the semicircle.

The diagram shows a region R on a coordinate grid.

Write down the three inequalities that define the region R.

The diagram shows the speed-time graph of a train journey between two stations from t = 0 s to t = 300 s.

(a) Find the acceleration of the train during the first 40 seconds.

(b) Calculate the distance between the two stations.

The table shows the amount of money, $x$, given to a charity by each of 60 people.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Amount ($x$) & Frequency \\
\hline
$0 \leq x < 20$ & 21 \\
$20 \leq x < 25$ & 16 \\
$25 \leq x < 35$ & 6 \\
$35 \leq x < 50$ & 10 \\
$50 \leq x < 100$ & 7 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Calculate an estimate of the mean.
texHtml:
The table shows the amount of money, $x$, given to a charity by each of 60 people.
Amount ($x$)Frequency
$0 \leq x < 20$21
$20 \leq x < 25$16
$25 \leq x < 35$6
$35 \leq x < 50$10
$50 \leq x < 100$7
Calculate an estimate of the mean.

tex:
Paddy and Anna each invest \$2000 for 5 years. Paddy earns simple interest at a rate of 1.25\% per year. Anna earns compound interest at a rate of $r\%$ per year. At the end of 5 years, Paddy's investment is worth the same as Anna's investment.

Calculate the value of $r$.

The diagram shows two shapes that are mathematically similar. The smaller shape has area 52.5 cm$^2$ and the larger shape has area 134.4 cm$^2$.

Calculate the value of $m$.

(a) Write $x^2 - 18x - 27$ in the form $(x + k)^2 + h$.

(b) Use your answer to part (a) to solve the equation $x^2 - 18x - 27 = 0$.

(a) In a class of 40 students:
\begin{parts}

(b) 28 wear glasses ($G$)

(c) 13 have driving lessons ($D$)

(d) 4 do not wear glasses and do not have driving lessons

(i) Complete the Venn diagram.

(ii) Use set notation to describe the region that contains a total of 32 students.

This Venn diagram shows information about the number of students who play basketball ($B$), football ($F$) and hockey ($H$):

\begin{tabular}{ccc}
& $B$ & \\
2 & 7 & 6 \\
& 5 & \\
3 & & 4 \\
& 8 & $H$ \\
& & 12 \\
\end{tabular}

Find $n((B \cup F) \cap H')$.

Shade the region $P \cap (Q \cup R)'$.

\end{parts}
texHtml:
(a) In a class of 40 students: \begin{parts} (b) 28 wear glasses ($G$) (c) 13 have driving lessons ($D$) (d) 4 do not wear glasses and do not have driving lessons (i) Complete the Venn diagram. (ii) Use set notation to describe the region that contains a total of 32 students. This Venn diagram shows information about the number of students who play basketball ($B$), football ($F$) and hockey ($H$):
$B$
276
5
34
8$H$
12
Find $n((B \cup F) \cap H')$. Shade the region $P \cap (Q \cup R)'$. \end{parts}

tex:
The diagram shows a pyramid with a square base ABCD. The diagonals AC and BD intersect at M. The vertex V is vertically above M. AB = 11 cm and AV = 18.6 cm.

Calculate the angle that AV makes with the base.

O is the origin and OPQR is a parallelogram. SOP is a straight line with SO = OP. TRQ is a straight line with TR = RQ. STV is a straight line and ST : TV = 2 : 1. $\overrightarrow{OR} = \mathbf{a}$ and $\overrightarrow{OP} = \mathbf{b}$.

(a) Find, in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$, in its simplest form,

(i) (b) the position vector of T,

(c) $\overrightarrow{RV}$.

(d) Show that PT is parallel to RV.
